checks.finding: The architecture names canonical data, deterministic generator, four native modules, runtime loaders, CI drift check, and the human-copy boundary; every component is listed with an owner technology.
The build-time canonical-to-generated flow is one-way and runtime loaders consume only native modules; no component feeds a guard decision back into canonical data.
The Data Plan defines the static integrity-critical canonical entity, existing duplicated constants, schema validation, immutable migration path, and no user-data migration.
The API/Contract Plan defines the PS comparator, evidence helper, risk checker diagnostics, generator modes, and their fail-closed behaviors.
Security boundaries cover protected command resolution, signed sudo verification, canonical-to-live integrity, and lite-to-full escalation with concrete OWASP concerns.
SKIP: this CLI/CI enforcement feature has no frontend; UX and frontend layer specifications explicitly record N/A.
Named TEST-001 through TEST-013 cover cross-runtime parity, PS5.1 static structure, golden contract output, policy selection, generator drift, loader behavior, and human-copy integrity.
All architecture nodes are defined in Components or are existing named repository surfaces; the shell dispatcher is explicitly provenance-only rather than a second decision engine.
The ADR Change Log records the user-selected scoped build-time generation decision and explains why no new durable ADR is required.
Design System Compliance explicitly records N/A for ds_profile none and no UI surface is introduced.
SKIP: no domain model applies to this enforcement-chain and workflow-hardening feature.
